title: CI scaffold: provenance linter + licence deny-list + 'no real data' scan
context: The no-data boundary and licence guardrails are only as strong as CI enforcement. The project needs three CI gates before any content work begins: (1) a provenance-completeness linter that fails the build on any administered item lacking a resolvable provenance link, (2) a licence deny-list scan that fails on embedded restricted terminology (SNOMED CT, ICD-O-3, LOINC, ICCR proprietary descriptions/hierarchy), and (3) a 'no real data' scan plus synthetic-attestation check that fails on any fixture lacking an attestation. Depends on rare-cancer-registry-templates-prov-001 and rare-cancer-registry-templates-license-001.
objective: Build the CI scaffold as a TypeScript/ESM pnpm package (or scripts) that provides: (1) a provenance-completeness linter checking every administered item in the repo against the provenance model from prov-001, (2) a licence deny-list scanner that flags any commit containing embedded descriptions or hierarchy from SNOMED CT, ICD-O-3, LOINC, or ICCR datasets, and (3) a 'no real data' scanner that checks every fixture file for a valid synthetic-data attestation per the format defined in gov-001. All three gates must run in CI (`pnpm build && pnpm test && pnpm lint` green).
acceptanceCriteria: CI fails on any administered item (CDE, vocab binding, crosswalk row, governance clause, regulatory claim) lacking a resolvable provenance link per the prov-001 model.
CI licence deny-list scan fails on any file containing embedded restricted terminology: descriptions or hierarchy from SNOMED CT, ICD-O-3, LOINC, or ICCR derivative content.
CI 'no real data' scan fails on any fixture lacking a valid synthetic-data attestation in the format defined by gov-001.
All three linters are integrated as pnpm scripts and run in CI; `pnpm build && pnpm test && pnpm lint` green on a clean repo with conforming content.
Failure messages are actionable: they identify the specific file and the missing/offending field.
The scaffold is agent-neutral (no vendor-specific logic); it lives in `tools/` or `ci/` following the agent-neutral core rules in CLAUDE.md.
output: A `tools/ci/` (or `ci/`) package with the provenance linter, licence deny-list scanner, and 'no real data' scanner integrated into pnpm CI scripts, committed via PR with green CI.
